title: Bayes' Theorem Application
content: If a disease affects 1% of the population, and a test is 99% accurate, what is the probability you have the disease if you test positive?

title: The Monty Hall Problem
content: You are on a game show and pick one of three doors. One has a car, two have goats. The host opens a goat door. Should you switch?

title: Law of Large Numbers
content: Explain the Law of Large Numbers and how it relates to casino profitability over time.

title: Normal Distribution
content: What is the 68-95-99.7 rule in statistics, and why is the normal distribution so common in nature?

title: Markov Chains
content: Define a Markov Chain. Give an example of a memoryless stochastic process.

title: Gambler's Fallacy
content: If a fair coin lands on heads 5 times in a row, what is the probability it will land on heads the 6th time? Explain the psychological bias.

title: Expected Value
content: A lottery ticket costs $2. There is a 1 in 1,000,000 chance to win $1,000,000. What is the expected value of the ticket?

title: Birthday Paradox
content: How many people do you need in a room to have a 50% chance that two people share the same birthday? Explain why.

title: Poisson Distribution
content: When is it appropriate to use a Poisson distribution instead of a Binomial distribution?

title: Standard Deviation vs Variance
content: What is the mathematical relationship between standard deviation and variance? Why do we use standard deviation more often in reports?
